headline: The CMO on the frontier: From AI experimentation to AI at work | The Microsoft Cloud Blog
article_title: The CMO on the frontier: From AI experimentation to AI at work | The Microsoft Cloud Blog

article_items.title: The CMO on the frontier: From AI experimentation to AI at work | The Microsoft Cloud Blog
article_items.description: CMOs are moving from AI experimentation to enterprise AI transformation. Learn how leaders operationalize AI for real impact across the business.
article_items.excerpt: CMOs are moving from AI experimentation to enterprise AI transformation. Learn how leaders operationalize AI for real impact across the business.
article_items.summary: That’s why the next phase of transformation isn’t about deploying AI around the business it’s about embedding it through the business. And can leaders scale AI without introducing risk to their brand? Customer and consumer engagement is not limited to human audiences, but LLMs and agents shaping discovery, consideration, and purchase in real time. Creating shared data and process standards to enable agents to operate reliably. That’s because the next chapter isn’t about experimenting with AI. The era of experimentation is over.

key_points: That’s why the next phase of transformation isn’t about deploying AI around the business it’s about embedding it through the business.
The era of experimentation is over.
And can leaders scale AI without introducing risk to their brand?
CMOs described teams juggling 25–30 disconnected applications, with AI pilots layered on top but rarely integrated end-to-end.
Discovery, consideration, and even purchase decisions are increasingly intermediated by AI agents disrupting traditional attribution models and forcing CMOs to rethink influence altogether.
Customer and consumer engagement is not limited to human audiences, but LLMs and agents shaping discovery, consideration, and purchase in real time.
And they need confidence in how to scale.
This introduces a profound shift: Brands must optimize not just for human attention, but for machine comprehension and recommendation.
Creating shared data and process standards to enable agents to operate reliably.
They’re accelerating the integration and operationalization of AI in an effort to rewire processes and supercharge their people.
But the real challenge runs deeper than the tech.
Leading companies are taking decisive action: Redesigning roles around human + agent collaboration, not task ownership Establishing clear governance models for AI decision-making and accountability.

article_items.title: Optimize blueprint extraction accuracy in Amazon Bedrock Data Automation | Amazon Web Services
article_items.description: Blueprint instruction optimization is a BDA feature that automatically refines your extraction instructions to address this challenge directly. You provide three to ten example documents with expected values, and BDA refines your blueprint instructions to improve accuracy in minutes, not weeks. No separate model fine-tuning is required. Run agent ( /evalkit.run_agent ) executes your agent against each test case, producing a structured trace file for every run that captures the full history of tool calls, model responses, and intermediate state.

Response Quality scored 83.9%, confirming that the agent produced clear, actionable travel advice, and Tool Parameter Accuracy reached 64.5%, showing the agent generally selected the right tools but sometimes passed.
Data ( /evalkit.data ) generates test cases grounded in the evaluation plan, each with inputs and expected outcomes targeting the specific behaviors and failure modes your agent needs to handle.

Howden's Group CDO on why the data product model breaks down at acquisition pace - and what an agent-ready data layer looks like instead The one-product-per-use-case model breaks down under acquisition-driven growth and.
The plan is the artifact, and the artifact is built to last.
Barry: When I arrived, we had roughly 80 data sources ingested, and it was taking about six months to integrate data post-acquisition.
The product model that fit a dashboard era is not the model that fits an agentic one.
At the pace we are acquiring, that meant people were creating silos or pulling data from elsewhere because they needed results now.
We can do cross-domain analytics, mixing client data, risk data and market data together, building it once instead of rebuilding per division.
And start thinking about data services, not just data products.

The dashboard backlog becomes the bottleneck.

article_items.title: I'm glad Apple isn't hyping up agentic AI (yet) - Engadget
article_items.description: Apple is more focused on delivering usable features with Siri AI, instead of hyping up agentic AI. That's a good thing.
article_items.excerpt: Apple is more focused on delivering usable features with Siri AI, instead of hyping up agentic AI. That's a good thing.
article_items.summary: We're also dealing with the first developer beta, so there's a lot of room for things to change between now and when Apple's latest OS updates drop this fall. Apple, meanwhile, has struggled with delays for its AI-powered Siri upgrade, which was first announced two years ago. For the most part, Siri AI responds to your commands, it just has the benefit of modern AI models to better synthesize data. And not surprisingly, it's also tied to one of the more troubling new Apple Intelligence features : The ability to automatically change your compromised passwords. It's easy to see how Apple could take this feature further: Maybe eventually AI could automatically purchase a hot product before it goes out of stock, or send an email to a group of friends if you're eyeing a vacation property's pricing. Apple is rarely the first to new technology, though.
